title: Modernising the census: Cabinet papers and key documents | Stats NZ
content:
## Modernising the census: Cabinet papers and key documents
12 March 2026, 5:08pm
The following Cabinet papers and ministerial correspondence outline the government’s decision-making processes to modernise the census, since 2012.
We have also provided other key documents that have supported and informed our work, including decision papers, reports, and notable research and methodology papers. These share further information to explain how and why the work to modernise the census has progressed, and demonstrate the range of insights and perspectives that have been considered.

## Cabinet papers and ministerial correspondence
Name of Cabinet paper
Data and Statistics (Census) Amendment Bill – Approval for introduction
Release of public consultation document – proposed data collection approach and content for the census
Modernising the Census by taking an administrative data-first approach
Modernising and transforming the 2028 Census: Public consultation on an administrative data-first approach for census
Confirmation of timing of New Zealand's next census of population and dwellings
Timing and nature of next census and update on use of administrative data
Census Transformation progress update - Realising the potential of Government data
Census Transformation - A promising future
Transforming the New Zealand census of population and dwellings: Issues, options and strategy

#### Government Statistician decision paper
Read the decision paper Approach to the manner of taking the next census considered by the Government Statistician in making the in-principle decision about the future of the census in Aotearoa New Zealand and how the next census will run.
Decision paper: Approach to the manner of taking the next census

#### Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel report and Stats NZ response
Read the report and recommendations of the Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel, which provided expert advice to the Government Statistician on the methodology and strategy we could use to modernise the census.
The link also features our response to the Panel’s recommendations.
Evaluation of New Zealand's Future Census Options for 2028 and Beyond: a report prepared by the Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel

#### Public consultation and engagement reports
Read the reports summarising the feedback we received through our public consultation process, customer and community engagement, and iwi and Māori engagement.
Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Summary of submissions
Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Summary of customer and community engagement
Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Summary of iwi and Māori engagement

#### Research and methodology papers
Since 2011, Stats NZ has published over 60 research papers on modernising the census, as part of our Census Transformation Research Programme. The table below highlights key research and methodology papers.
Visit the programme page for more information, and to access our broader census research.
Technical feasibility: Measuring population and dwellings using administrative data and 2025 – an annual update going forward
Using a combined census model for the 2023 Census (contains the methodology papers for the 2023 Census, published in 2022 and 2024)
Experimental administrative population census: data sources and methods and 2022 and 2023 The quality of administrative data for census variables: Strengths, limitations, and opportunities
Overview of statistical methods for adding admin records to the 2018 Census dataset
The potential for linked administrative data to provide household and family information
Experimental population estimates from linked administrative data: 2017 release and again in 2018 Experimental population estimates from linked administrative data
Identifying the New Zealand resident population in the Integrated Data Infrastructure Identifying Māori populations using administrative data: A comparison with the census
Enduring census information requirements for and about Māori
Quality standards for population statistics: accuracy requirements for future census models
An overview of progress on the potential use of administrative data for census information in New Zealand: Census Transformation programme
Options for future New Zealand censuses: Census Transformation programme
Transforming the New Zealand Census of Population and Dwellings: Issues, options, and strategy
Future New Zealand censuses: Implications of changing census frequency or adopting other models
